heading: Diplomatic and semidiplomatic immunities
label: 1102 Diplomatic and semidiplomatic immunities

Denial of Visas to Certain Representatives to United Nations, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–246, title IV, § 407104 Stat. 67Pub. L. 113–100, § 1128 Stat. 1145Feb. 16, 1990Apr. 18, 2014“(a).—In GeneralThe President shall use his authority, including the authorities contained in section 6 of the United Nations Headquarters Agreement Act () [, set out as a note under ], to deny any individual’s admission to the United States as a representative to the United Nations if the President determines that such individual—Public Law 80–357Aug. 4, 1947, ch. 48222 U.S.C. 287“(1)has been found to have been engaged in espionage activities or a terrorist activity (as defined in section 212(a)(3)(B)(iii) of the Immigration and Nationality Act ()) directed against the United States or its allies; and8 U.S.C. 1182(a)(3)(B)(iii)“(2)may pose a threat to United States national security interests.“(b).—WaiverThe President may waive the provisions of subsection (a) if the President determines, and so notifies the Congress, that such a waiver is in the national security interests of the United States.”
